ddls:
CREATE TABLE "crimes" (
  "crime_id" TEXT /* Unique identifier for each crime incident */,
  "month" TEXT /* Month and year the crime was reported */,
  "reported_by" TEXT /* Police department that reported the crime */,
  "falls_within" TEXT /* Area where the crime was committed */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the crime location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the crime location */,
  "location" TEXT /* Specific location where the crime was committed */,
  "crimetype" TEXT /* Type of crime (e.g., theft, anti-social behavior) */,
  "outcome" TEXT /* Outcome of the investigation (e.g., arrest, conviction, unresolved) */,
  "context" TEXT /* Additional context about the crime (e.g., suspect information) */,
  "crime_date" TEXT /* Exact date the crime was reported */,
  "crime_time" TEXT /* Time the crime was reported */,
  "incident_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the incident */,
  "suspect_description" TEXT /* Description of the suspect(s) */,
  "evidence_collected" TEXT /* Details of the evidence collected */,
  "assigned_officer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the officer assigned to the case */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the investigation (e.g., open, closed) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("crime_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crimes_location FOREIGN KEY ("location") REFERENCES locations ("location_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crimes_assigned_officer_id FOREIGN KEY ("assigned_officer_id") REFERENCES officers ("officer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crimes_reported_by FOREIGN KEY ("reported_by") REFERENCES police_departments ("department_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crimes_crimetype FOREIGN KEY ("crimetype") REFERENCES crime_types ("type_name"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_crimes_outcome FOREIGN KEY ("outcome") REFERENCES investigation_outcomes ("outcome_name")
)

CREATE TABLE "crime_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each crime category */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the crime category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the crime category */,
  "severity_level" TEXT /* Severity level of the crime (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "is_violent" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the crime is violent */,
  "is_property_related" INTEGER /* Indicates whether the crime involves property */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "police_departments" (
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each police department */,
  "department_name" TEXT /* Name of the police department */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for the police department */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the police department */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the police department */,
  "jurisdiction" TEXT /* Jurisdiction area covered by the department */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("department_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "location_name" TEXT /* Name of the location */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the location */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the location */,
  "neighborhood" TEXT /* Neighborhood where the location is situated */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "zip_code" TEXT /* ZIP code of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "investigation_outcomes" (
  "outcome_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each investigation outcome */,
  "outcome_name" TEXT /* Name of the investigation outcome */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the investigation outcome */,
  "date_of_resolution" TEXT /* Date when the investigation was resolved */,
  "resolution_details" TEXT /* Additional details about the resolution */,
  "suspect_id" INTEGER /* ID of the suspect (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("outcome_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "officers" (
  "officer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each police officer */,
  "officer_name" TEXT /* Name of the police officer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the police officer */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the police officer (e.g., detective, patrol officer) */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number of the officer */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* ID of the department the officer belongs to */,
  "rank" TEXT /* Rank of the officer (e.g., sergeant, lieutenant) */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit the officer is assigned to (e.g., homicide, narcotics) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("officer_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_officers_department_id FOREIGN KEY ("department_id") REFERENCES police_departments ("department_id")
)
